name: IONOS Cloud API
what_it_does: European cloud infrastructure provider with REST API — enabling businesses to provision virtual data centers, compute instances, managed Kubernetes, object storage, databases, load balancers, and bare metal servers across European and US data centers, with strong German data privacy compliance and GDPR-first design.
use_cases: Provisioning IONOS Cloud virtual data centers and servers via API for infrastructure automation
Managing IONOS managed Kubernetes clusters via API for k8s infrastructure operations
Configuring IONOS object storage (S3-compatible) via API for data storage automation
Managing IONOS load balancers and networking via API for infrastructure-as-code workflows
Querying IONOS server metrics and billing data via API for cost monitoring
not_for: AWS/Azure/GCP managed service breadth (IONOS is IaaS/cloud infrastructure focused)
Developer PaaS deployment (use Railway, Render, or Fly.io for PaaS)
Serverless compute beyond VMs and containers
best_when: A European SME or enterprise needs GDPR-compliant, German-jurisdiction cloud infrastructure at competitive pricing — with virtual data centers, managed Kubernetes, and strong DSGVO (German GDPR) compliance.
avoid_when: You need AWS-equivalent managed services, developer PaaS, or are outside Europe where IONOS has less infrastructure.

auth.notes: Bearer token auth. Tokens obtained via POST /auth/login with credentials or via API key. HTTP Basic auth for v5 (deprecated). OAuth2 via IONOS identity provider. Token-based auth for API v6. No webhooks — must poll for operation status. SDKs for Go, Python, Java, Ansible, Terraform provider. Managed Kubernetes uses kubeconfig.

pricing.paid_tiers: VPS from €1/month (cloud); Dedicated cloud from €15/month; Managed k8s from €0.0096/hr/node; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: German company (Karlsruhe). Formerly 1&1 Internet. United Internet subsidiary. NASDAQ-listed parent (UI). €1.2B+ revenue. 12 million customers. Data centers in Germany, UK, USA, Spain, France. IONOS Cloud (enterprise) separate from shared hosting. Virtual Data Center (VDC) model — layer 2 isolation. ISO27001, TISAX (automotive), C5 (BSI German cloud compliance). DSGVO/GDPR compliant. Strong German Mittelstand (SME) market. Managed Kubernetes, managed databases (MongoDB, PostgreSQL, MySQL, Redis). Object storage (S3-compatible). DDoS protection included.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API v6 with OpenAPI documentation. Bearer token auth. SDKs for Go, Python, Java, Ansible. Terraform provider. No webhooks — must poll. GDPR, ISO27001, C5 compliant. German jurisdiction. Rate limits not documented. Virtual Data Center model requires understanding VDC concept.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Server reads idempotent by ID. Network reads idempotent. VDC reads idempotent. Server creation not idempotent — check by name. Operation status reads idempotent by request ID.
known_agent_gotchas: Virtual Data Center (VDC) model — provision VDC first, then resources within VDC
No webhooks — must poll for async operation completion
API v5 used HTTP Basic auth — v6 uses bearer tokens (different pattern)
C5 (BSI) certification covers specific data center regions — verify for regulated workloads
Rate limits not documented — test before high-frequency automation

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR, ISO27001, TISAX, BSI C5 compliant. Bearer token auth with expiry. German jurisdiction (strictest EU privacy). OAuth2 for identity. No webhooks reduces outbound data exposure. VDC model provides network isolation. DDoS protection included. C5 certification for cloud security standards. DSGVO compliance for German SME market.
